current_calibration.plus_ssh_setup_estimate_seconds: 3-8 per attempt (ConnectTimeout=8 default, ServerAliveInterval=30) → ceiling ~10-15s end-to-end before chain advance
current_calibration.retry_eligibility: stderr regex (Connection timed out / Operation timed out / Network unreachable / No route to host / Connection reset / Connection closed by / kex_exchange_identification / banner exchange / Could not resolve hostname / Name or service not known) OR ssh rc=255 (transport)

ssh_blip_typical_timing.banner_handshake_recovery: kex_exchange_identification / banner exchange typically self-heals in 1-3s on sshd restart or transient packet loss
ssh_blip_typical_timing.tcp_syn_loss_recovery: Linux default first-SYN retransmit at ~1s, second at ~3s, total ConnectTimeout=8s ceiling — 1s backoff is well-aligned
ssh_blip_typical_timing.dns_recovery: resolv.conf flap or upstream NS blip typically resolves <500ms; 1s first-backoff comfortably covers
ssh_blip_typical_timing.routing_reconvergence: BGP/route-flap on midnetwork can take 10-30s — current 7s ceiling DOES NOT cover; would need attempt 4 (8s) + attempt 5 (16s) = 31s total. Empirically rare on hetzner Frankfurt link

fallthrough_cost_vs_retry_cost.fallthrough_path: _probe_host (300ms TCP probe) + ssh setup on next host (~3-8s) + repeat REMOTE_CMD setup (cd, exports). For heavy-compute already routed to hetzner, fall-through to ubu1/ubu2 means RAM cap MemoryMax=20G vs 80G → likely rc=137 OOM downstream. Fall-through is EXPENSIVE for heavy class.
fallthrough_cost_vs_retry_cost.retry_path: Same host, no rsync repeat (REMOTE_CMD already built), 1-7s wall + new ssh setup ~3-8s. Cheaper than chain advance for heavy class.
fallthrough_cost_vs_retry_cost.verdict: current calibration is biased correctly for heavy-compute (favor retry over fall-through). Lights bypass means lights workloads would tolerate either.

audit_findings.max_retries_3_assessment: Adequate for SSH-banner / TCP-SYN / DNS-flap classes. Marginal for routing reconvergence (~30s class). Raising to 5 (12s + 16s = +28s wall) would cover routing but doubles worst-case latency budget on dead hosts; preflight already filters most dead hosts so the hit is rare.
audit_findings.missing_data: no /tmp/hexa_remote_reconnect.log existed prior to this cycle. No empirical histogram of (attempt_n, success?) — cannot data-drive A vs B calibration.

A_extend_max_to_5.con: +28s worst case on truly dead hosts; doubles latency budget; no empirical evidence routing-flap is happening

B_tighten_to_0.5_1_2.con: 0.5s likely under-shoots TCP first-SYN retransmit (~1s); for heavy-compute fall-through is expensive (RAM-class mismatch); BSD sleep(1) accepts fractional but POSIX sleep does not — portability snag

C_instrument_log_no_change.change: append TSV per attempt to /tmp/hexa_remote_reconnect.log; behavior unchanged
C_instrument_log_no_change.pro: zero behavior risk; unblocks data-driven future calibration; raw#41 spirit (instrument first, tune second)
C_instrument_log_no_change.con: defers decision; small disk write per ssh dispatch

calibration_chosen.rationale: No empirical data exists on retry success distribution. Tuning A or B would be guess-driven. Option C instruments the function with structured TSV logging so a future cycle (omega-network-1.next, scheduled when log accumulates ≥50 retry events) can read the histogram of (err_class, attempt_n, outcome) and decide A vs B with evidence. Conservative engineering: measure first, tune second.

changes_applied.before_summary: Single while loop: ssh → check rc → check stderr regex → sleep → repeat. No structured logging; only stderr human-readable line on retry.
changes_applied.after_summary: Same control flow + nested _recon_log_emit helper appending TSV per outcome. New env: HEXA_REMOTE_RECONNECT_LOG (default 1), HEXA_REMOTE_RECONNECT_LOG_PATH (default /tmp/hexa_remote_reconnect.log). Outcomes logged: SUCCESS, RETRY, EXHAUSTED, NONRETRY_EXIT, DISABLED. err_class enum: NONE/TIMEOUT/UNREACHABLE/RESET/KEX/DNS/OTHER.
changes_applied.tsv_schema: iso_ts \t host \t attempt_n \t max \t rc \t err_class \t outcome \t backoff_s
changes_applied.behavior_invariant: TRUE — backoff sequence (1,2,4), max=3, regex match set, retry/non-retry decisions all unchanged. Log emit failures swallowed (|| true) so dispatch path can never break on disk-full / perm error.

validation.unit_test_emit: extracted _recon_log_emit into isolated test, fired 5 synthetic events (SUCCESS, RETRY×2, EXHAUSTED, NONRETRY_EXIT) — all emitted correct TSV rows with ISO timestamp
validation.unit_test_disabled_branch: extracted DISABLED branch with mocked _run_ssh_once returning 64 — emitted single DISABLED row, returned rc=64. Verified emit reachable on the early-return path.
validation.live_ssh_smoke: DEFERRED per constraint 'NO live ssh hammering'. The function is invoked on every hexa_remote dispatch — next legitimate hexa call to hetzner/ubu1 will exercise the SUCCESS path and append one row. Bogus-host test deferred to user discretion (would emit EXHAUSTED row).
validation.behavior_diff_proof: no edits to backoff math (_backoff=1, *=2), no edits to _max default (3), no edits to regex set, no edits to ssh -o flags. Only additive: helper function + log-emit calls + variable initialization.

rollback.method_a_revert_block: git diff HEAD -- nexus/scripts/bin/hexa_remote then git checkout HEAD -- nexus/scripts/bin/hexa_remote (uncommitted change as of this cycle)
rollback.method_b_runtime_disable: export HEXA_REMOTE_RECONNECT_LOG=0 — disables emit without code change; backoff behavior unaffected (was unchanged anyway)
rollback.method_c_log_purge: rm -f /tmp/hexa_remote_reconnect.log — log is append-only; safe to delete at any time

next_cycle_trigger.name: omega-network-1.next (data-driven calibration)
next_cycle_trigger.precondition: /tmp/hexa_remote_reconnect.log accumulates ≥50 lines with outcome ∈ {RETRY, EXHAUSTED, SUCCESS}
next_cycle_trigger.analysis_recipe: awk -F'\t' '{print $6,$7}' | sort | uniq -c — get (err_class, outcome) histogram. If RETRY dominates with attempt_n=1-2 SUCCESS within 3 retries, keep current. If EXHAUSTED with attempt_n=3 frequent → option A (extend to 5). If RETRY rare and chain-advance frequent on first failure → option B (tighten).
next_cycle_trigger.estimated_data_arrival: ~1-2 weeks of normal hexa_remote usage at observed dispatch frequency

constraints_honored.edits_scope: ONLY /Users/ghost/core/nexus/scripts/bin/hexa_remote — confirmed via single Edit call
constraints_honored.no_live_ssh_hammering: TRUE — only isolated unit tests with mocked _run_ssh_once; no actual ssh invocation
constraints_honored.no_git_commit: TRUE — change left uncommitted in working tree
